name: Bloom filter
task: Create /tmp/eval_bloom/bloom.py with class BloomFilter(capacity, error_rate). Compute optimal bit-array size m = ceil(-(capacity * ln(error_rate)) / (ln 2)^2) and number of hash functions k = round((m / capacity) * ln 2). Methods: add(item) and contains(item) — item is a str; hash its UTF-8 encoding. contains returns True if maybe-present, False if definitely-not. Back the filter with an actual bit array of length m (e.g. a bytearray or list of 0/1), NOT a Python set of the items. Use double hashing (hashlib.md5 and hashlib.sha256 of the item's UTF-8 bytes, combined as h1 + i*h2, each index taken mod m). Expose the integer attributes m (bit-array size) and k (number of hash functions).

validation.script: import importlib.util; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m','/tmp/eval_bloom/bloom.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod); bf = mod.BloomFilter(1000, 0.01); items = [f'user_{i}' for i in range(500)]; [bf.add(it) for it in items]; assert all(bf.contains(it) for it in items); fps = sum(1 for i in range(10000) if bf.contains(f'absent_{i}')); assert fps/10000 < 0.05, f'fp rate {fps/10000}'; assert bf.m == 9586, f'm formula: got {bf.m}'; assert bf.k == 7, f'k formula: got {bf.k}'; print('OK')
